notes: Chunk 76 DONE (Template A, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block) + Blog Day 29. Opened a brand new Tier B cluster: 7 countries with no prior route coverage and no country-guide page yet, crossed with the established hub-market set (UK/US/AU/CA/UAE). Maldives (8 routes: to/from UK, US, Australia, UAE): verified via the Maldives Customs Service's own prohibited-import list that dogs are banned outright from entry, no standard exception process; cats are permitted with rabies vaccination, core feline vaccines and a health certificate, though permit/titre-test guidance is thin and inconsistently reported, so every Maldives page states that honestly rather than asserting false certainty; the maldives-to-australia route is the standout, since Australia treats the Maldives as a non-approved country, forcing a 180-day residency stopover in an approved country before DAFF/BICON and Mickleham quarantine apply (9-14 months total). Macau (2 routes, Canada both directions): confirmed Macau's own quarantine authority (IAM) dropped its mandatory 30-day quarantine around June 2026, and that Canada's CFIA high-risk dog-rabies designation for China explicitly excludes Macau, Hong Kong and Taiwan. Brunei (4 routes, UK/US both directions): confirmed Brunei's scheduled-country system exempts UK-resident pets from the titre test and facility quarantine that US-origin pets still face, and that Royal Brunei Airlines carries no unaccompanied pet cargo, so every route transits Singapore or Kuala Lumpur. Laos (4 routes, UK/US both directions): confirmed via the CDC's 15 April 2026 high-risk list that Laos requires the same FAVN-titre-test process as Cambodia/Myanmar/Vietnam, but verified Laos-specific facts independently (Department of Livestock and Fisheries, Wattay International's actual connecting-hub network) rather than copying a sibling country's details. Seychelles (3 routes, UK/US, UK outbound): confirmed Seychelles' own rabies-free status does not ease entry (mandatory quarantine for every arriving pet regardless of origin) nor exit to the UK (unlisted under GB's pet travel scheme, full titre test and 3-month wait), while exit to the US is genuinely light (absent from the CDC high-risk list, CDC Dog Import Form only). Trinidad and Tobago (3 routes, UK/US, UK outbound): corrected an assumption that Caribbean Airlines flies to the UK (it ended that route in 2016; British Airways/IAG Cargo is the sole nonstop carrier), and confirmed Trinidad and Tobago's own APHD import process requires a Brucella canis test for intact dogs alongside the FAVN titre test. Uzbekistan (1 route, UK outbound): confirmed no rabies titre test is required for UK-origin pets given a compliant vaccination window, but flagged that import-permit guidance is genuinely ambiguous across sources and treated the stricter reading as the safe assumption. All 25 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary, valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block, correct Marcus Webb persona, no placeholder strings, no contact email/phone stated). Blog Day 29: uk-to-france-pet-transport-guide.md replaced in place (author changed from Dr Sarah Okafor to Marcus Webb to match route-logistics content), full rewrite from a thin 450-word 2026-05-06 stub to approximately 2,200 words, correcting a genuine factual error present in the old version: France itself has no tapeworm-treatment requirement for dogs, that rule only applies on the return leg into Great Britain. Routes 6,321 -> 6,346, blog unchanged at 431 (replace in place), content plan articles 28 -> 29, pages 7,126 -> 7,151. Chunk 77 (Template B, Tier B) is next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
